import sys

def matrix_chain_multiplication(dims):
    n = len(dims) - 1  # Number of matrices

    # dp[i][j] stores the minimum number of scalar multiplications needed
    # to multiply the chain of matrices from index i to j.
    # Matrix A_k has dimensions dims[k] x dims[k+1].
    dp = [[0 for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]

    # L is the chain length. It varies from 2 to n.
    for L in range(2, n + 1):
        # i is the starting index of the matrix chain.
        for i in range(n - L + 1):
            j = i + L - 1  # j is the ending index of the matrix chain.
            dp[i][j] = sys.maxsize  # Initialize with a very large value

            # k is the split point, dividing the chain into two sub-chains:
            # (A_i ... A_k) and (A_{k+1} ... A_j)
            for k in range(i, j):
                # cost = (cost of multiplying A_i to A_k)
                #        + (cost of multiplying A_{k+1} to A_j)
                #        + (cost of multiplying the two resulting matrices)
                cost = dp[i][k] + dp[k+1][j] + dims[i] * dims[k+1] * dims[j+1]
                if cost < dp[i][j]:
                    dp[i][j] = cost

    return dp[0][n - 1]
